content.claim: Hungary-specific panel data study finds early selective tracks have higher value-added in literacy and mathematics and higher tertiary progression, with higher-status student composition, leading to increased inequality of opportunity; evidence is weaker that students in general tracks lose, but this tendency is suggested
content.context: Fresh live research through the International comparison lens
limitations: Value-added estimates depend on specification choices and controls for prior achievement
Panel study covers specific cohort(s) and may not generalize to current population
Causal identification relies on selection-on-observables assumptions that may not hold
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
